message:
fix(share): harden bundle security, normalize GitHub repo detection, and deduplicate git remote calls

Bundle:
- Narrow attachment error degradation to NotFound only; policy violations
  (root escape, absolute path) and invalid sources now fail the export
- Change PermissionDenied to io::Error::other for path-escape errors so
  they propagate as hard failures instead of silent degradation
- Resolve symlinks during collection and reject those escaping source
  tree; include in-tree symlink targets instead of silently skipping
- Add collect_entries_ctx() with context label for better error messages

Crypto:
- Check for existing .age output file before invoking age binary
- Move check_age_available() after output-exists guard to fail fast
- Add test for refusing to overwrite existing encrypted output

Detection:
- Normalize GitHub repo extraction to owner/repo only (strip trailing
  path segments like /tree/main, /issues/123, query strings)
- Use find_ancestor_path for docs/ and scripts/ directory detection
  so nested bundle output directories still find project-level signals
- Factor out apply_github_env_vars() and detect_github_pages_with_remote()
  to avoid redundant git-remote subprocess calls
- Add tests for ancestor detection and env var handling

Hosting:
- Hoist git_remote_url() call to detect_hosting_hints() and pass result
  through detect_github_pages/cloudflare/netlify to eliminate redundant
  subprocess invocations

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
